title: ACT ON THE OPERATION AND PROCESSING OF 112 EMERGENCY REPORTS
text:
CHAPTER I GENERAL PROVISIONS

Article 1 (Purpose) The purpose of this Act is to protect the lives, physical safety, and property of the people from crimes, various incidents, accidents, and other emergency situations, and to maintain public peace and order by prescribing matters relating to the operation and processing of 112 emergency reports.

Article 2 (Definitions) The terms used in this Act are defined as follows: 
  1. The term "112" means a special number 112 assigned in accordance with the management plans for telecommunications number resources under Article 48 of the Telecommunications Business Act;
  2. The term "report to 112" means a report made by a victim or any person who becomes aware of, or anticipates, an emergency situation, such as a crime, incident, or accident, through voice or text via 112, and through other means such as the Internet, video, or smart devices, when such an emergency situation has occurred or is expected to occur.

Article 3 (Responsibilities of the State) (1) The State shall establish a system for the prompt and effective processing of, and response to, a report to 112.
  (2) The State shall establish and operate a cooperative system among relevant agencies to jointly respond to reports to 112.
  (3) The State shall ensure accessibility to reports to 112 so that no one is disadvantaged in using such reports due to disability, language barrier, or any other reason.

Article 4 (Rights and duties of the people) (1) Any person shall have the right to request a prompt response from the State using reports to 112 when an emergency situation, such as a crime, an incident, or accident, has occurred or is likely to occur.
  (2) No person shall report to 112 for any purpose other than to respond to an emergency situation, such as a crime, an incident, or accident, nor shall any person falsely report to 112.

Article 5 (Relationship to other statutes) Except as otherwise expressly provided in other statutes, this Act shall apply to the operation and processing of reports to 112.

CHAPTER II RECEIPT AND PROCESSING OF REPORTS TO 112

Article 6 (Establishment and Operation of 112 Emergency Dispatching and Operations Command Center) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, the commissioner of a City/Do police agency, and the chief of a police station (hereinafter referred to as the "Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc.") shall establish and operate a 112 Emergency Dispatching and Operations Command Center to promptly receive and process reports to 112 and to analyze, judge, disseminate, share, and transfer information on reports to 112 for such purposes, to manage situations, to direct, coordinate, and control the scene, and to jointly respond to such reports.
  (2) Other matters necessary for the establishment and operation of the 112 Emergency Dispatching and Operations Command Center shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 7 (Receipt of reports to 112) (1) Upon receiving reports to 112, the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. shall promptly receive and process the reports, regardless of the classification of police affairs under Article 4 (1) of the Act on the Organization and Operation of National Police and Autonomous Police or the jurisdiction over the area requiring on-site dispatch.
  (2) No person shall, without good cause, obstruct the receipt and processing of reports to 112 under paragraph (1) by deceit, abuse of authority, violence, intimidation, etc.
  (3) Matters necessary for the receipt and processing of reports to 112 under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 8 (Measures in response to reports to 112) (1) Upon receipt of reports to 112 under Article 7 (1), the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. shall promptly dispatch police officers to the scene to take necessary measures, such as the prevention of danger, prevention and suppression of crimes, and the rescue of persons in need of assistance.
  (2) If a police officer has taken necessary measures under paragraph (1), he or she shall, without delay, refer the relevant reports to 112 to the relevant investigative agency if there are reasonable grounds to suspect that a crime has been committed and further investigation is required.
  (3) If a police offer, in taking necessary measures under paragraph (1), deems it unavoidable to prevent imminent harm to human life, physical safety, or property, or to rescue a victim, he or she may, to the extent reasonably necessary, temporarily use, restrict the use of, or dispose of, another person's land, building, or other property, or enter another person's land, building, vessel, or vehicle. 
  (4) If the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. deem that a disaster, calamity, crime, or other emergency situation occurring in the course of processing reports to 112 is likely to endanger human life or physical safety, they may designate a certain area and order persons within such area to evacuate therefrom.
  (5) If a police officer enters a place or takes other measures under paragraph (3), he or she shall present identification verifying his or her status, state his or her affiliation and name, and explain the purpose of, and reasons for, such measures.
  (6) If any person suffers loss due to any measure or order under paragraph (1), (3), or (4), the State shall compensate for such loss pursuant to Article 11-2 of the Act on the Performance of Duties by Police Officers.

Article 9 (Joint response or cooperation) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. may request joint response or cooperation from other agencies when such cooperation is necessary for processing reports to 112. Upon receipt of such request, the head of the agency shall cooperate unless there is a compelling reason not to do so.
  (2) Upon receipt of a request for joint response or cooperation under paragraph (1), the relevant agencies shall promptly and safely take necessary measures, such as the prevention of danger, the prevention and suppression of crimes, and the rescue of persons in need of assistance.
  (3) If a relevant agency that has taken necessary measures under paragraph (2) determines that there are reasonable grounds to suspect a crime in connection with the reports to 112 and further investigation is required, it shall, without delay, refer the matter to the relevant investigative agency.
  (4) Requests for joint response and cooperation, measures taken by relevant agencies, and referral to investigative agencies under paragraphs (1) through (3), and other necessary matters shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 10 (Protection of persons who reported to 112) (1) When processing a report to 112, the State shall protect the person who reported to 112 (hereinafter referred to as the "person who reported to 112") if he or she is a victim of a crime (including crimes that have already been committed, are in progress, or are deemed imminent; hereinafter the same shall apply), a witness to a crime, or a person who has requested rescue in an emergency situation such as an incident or accident.
  (2) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. shall not collect, use, or provide the telephone number used for reports to 112, the name, address, gender, age, voice of the person who reported to 112, and any other information that can be used to identify or infer the person who reported to 112 (hereinafter referred to as "information on the person who reported to 112"), except in any of the following cases:
  1. Where the information on the person who reported to 112 is used for the processing of the report to 112;
  2. Where the person who reported to 112 consents to such collection, use, or provision;
  3. Where special provisions exist in this Act or other statutes.
  (3) No person shall use the information on the person who reported to 112 under paragraph (2) for any purpose other than the receipt and processing of the report to 112.
  (4) The collection, use, or provision of the information on the person who reported to 112 under the subparagraphs of paragraph (2) shall be limited to the minimum extent necessary for the performance of the relevant duties.

Article 11 (Recording and management of dispatch sites) (1) When processing reports to 112, the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. may install video recording devices on patrol cars, etc. to record the dispatch site for the purpose of checking and supervising the situation at the site in real time at the 112 Emergency Dispatching and Operations Command Center.
  (2) The period, methods, and procedures for the storage, use, and destruction of video information collected under paragraph (1) and other necessary matters shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.
  (3) Except as provided in this Act, the Personal Information Protection Act shall apply to matters relating to the protection and management of video information recorded under paragraph (1).

Article 12 (Recording and retention of reports to 112) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. shall record and retain the status of receipt and processing of reports to 112 by entering, audio recording, video recording, etc. such information in the 112 system under Article 13.
  (2) Matters necessary for the method and scope of recording the status of receipt and processing of reports to 112 under paragraph (1), the retention period, management, destruction, etc. shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

CHAPTER III ESTABLISHMENT AND OPERATION OF 112 SYSTEM

Article 13 (Establishment and operation of 112 system) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency shall establish and operate an information system (hereinafter referred to as the "112 system") necessary for the receipt and processing of reports to 112, the sharing and transfer of information on reports to 112, joint responses, etc.
  (2) Matters necessary for the establishment and operation of a 112 system under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 14 (Linkage with other information systems) (1) If it is necessary to share information, etc. on reports to 112 to process the reports for the protection of the life, physical safety, and property of a person in urgent need of assistance, the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency and the commissioner of a City/Do police agency may request the head of relevant agencies to link the 112 system with the information system of the relevant agencies.
  (2) If the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency and the commissioner of the City/Do police agency request the linkage of the information systems under paragraph (1), they shall have a prior consultation with the heads of relevant agencies.
  (3) Matters necessary for the standards, methods, and procedures for linking the information systems referred to in paragraph (1), relevant agencies, the scope of linked information, etc. shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

CHAPTER IV SUPPLEMENTARY PROVISIONS

Article 15 (Education, training, and public relations) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency shall operate education and training programs necessary for training professional human resources and improving their skills related to the operation of the 112 system.
  (2) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. shall, if necessary to improve the convenience and to facilitate the use, of reports to 112, conduct public relations activities.
  (3) Matters necessary for the operation of education and training programs under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 16 (Award for persons who reported to 112) (1) The Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc. may grant an award or provide a monetary award within the budget, to any person who has made significant contributions to preventing crimes and protecting the life, physical safety, or property of others through reports to 112.
  (2) Details regarding persons eligible for awards and monetary awards under paragraph (1), and the standards, methods, procedures, etc. for the payment thereof shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

CHAPTER V PENALTY PROVISIONS

Article 17 (Penalty provisions) Any person who uses information on the person who reported to 112 for any purpose other than the intended purpose, in violation of Article 10 (3), shall be punished by imprisonment with labor for not more than 5 years or by a fine not exceeding 50 million won.

Article 18 (Administrative fines) (1) Any person who falsely reports to 112, in violation of Article 4 (2), by falsely describing an emergency situation, such as a crime, an incident, or accident shall be subject to an administrative fine not exceeding 5 million won.
  (2) Any person who refuses or obstructs the temporary use, restriction on use, or disposal of land, goods, etc., or access to land, buildings, ships, or vehicles under Article 8 (3) without good cause shall be subject to an administrative fine not exceeding 3 million won.
  (3) Any person who violates an evacuation order issued under Article 8 (4) without good cause shall be subject to an administrative fine not exceeding 1 million won.
  (4) Administrative fines under paragraphs (1) through (3) shall be imposed and collected by the Commissioner General of the Korean National Police Agency, etc., as prescribed by Presidential Decree.

ADDENDUM <Act No. 19870, Jan. 2, 2024>
This Act shall enter into force 6 months after the date of its promulgation.
